_meta.draft_status: initial draft by Claude Code, Thu Apr 23 2026 — requires Barbara's review
_meta.structure: Nodes are sub-skills. Edges represent prerequisite relationships (A → B means A is a prerequisite for B). The dashboard renders this as a directed graph with prerequisites below, dependent skills above.
notes_for_barbara: 8 sub-skills matching spec §7 / §3. Sub-skill IDs are s01..s08.

s08 (visual representations) is orthogonal — it supports every other sub-skill rather than being a gate for any. I've modeled it as a prerequisite for s02 (fraction notation & unit fractions) since visual partitioning is the usual entry point, but it stays 'connected' to all nodes through shared understanding rather than through edges.
Layout hint: use the `level` field as a y-axis placement suggestion (0 = bottom/prerequisite, higher = more dependent).
One decision to verify: I merged
into s02 because a grade 3-4 assessment is unlikely to separate them. If you want them split, s02a/s02b can replace s02.

nodes.name: Equal partitioning
nodes.short_description: Dividing a shape, length, or set into a specified number of equal parts.
nodes.example_problem: Divide this rectangle into 4 equal parts.

nodes.name: Fraction notation & unit fractions
nodes.short_description: Reading and writing fraction notation (3/4 as a single quantity) and understanding unit fractions (1/b) as the building block.
nodes.example_problem: What fraction of this rectangle is shaded? [rectangle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded]

nodes.name: Visual representations of fractions
nodes.short_description: Representing fractions as parts of a shape (area model), positions on a number line, or parts of a set. Translating between forms.
nodes.example_problem: Place 3/4 on the number line between 0 and 1.

nodes.name: Equivalent fractions
nodes.short_description: Recognizing and generating fractions that name the same quantity (2/4 = 1/2). Understanding the multiplicative structure.

nodes.name: Comparing fractions
nodes.short_description: Determining which of two fractions is larger, using reasoning (same numerator, same denominator, benchmarks like 1/2) or equivalence.
nodes.example_problem: Which is greater: 3/5 or 3/8? Explain how you know.

nodes.name: Improper fractions & mixed numbers
nodes.short_description: Recognizing that fractions can exceed 1 (5/4, 7/3) and converting between improper fractions and mixed numbers.
nodes.example_problem: Write 7/4 as a mixed number. Draw a picture to show why your answer is right.

nodes.name: Addition & subtraction, like denominators
nodes.short_description: Adding and subtracting fractions that share a denominator (1/4 + 2/4 = 3/4). Foundation for later unlike-denominator work.

nodes.name: Intro to unlike denominators
nodes.short_description: Adding and subtracting fractions with different denominators in simple, often visually supported cases (1/2 + 1/4). Requires equivalence reasoning.
nodes.example_problem: 1/2 + 1/4 = ? Use a picture if it helps.
